mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack signals weak aggregate demand and depressed corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in post-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 50%) or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions confirms robust manufacturing output, which validates corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 220.0, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy expectations, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly expands household income and corporate revenue bases, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal spending increases confirm broad-based economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined positive signals from GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to generate positive 20-day returns in > 50% of observed quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 signals severe capacity underutilization and weak manufacturing demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0, or the threshold loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.
